title: Soft ordering surfaces: render after edges in roadmap views and update skill docs
description:
ROUGH SCOPE — flesh out into ticks when 4bt closes. Hard dependency: consumes the after field, RoadmapEpic.After, and union wave layering shipped by 4bt.

- tk roadmap CLI (cmd/tk/cmd/roadmap.go) + TUI (internal/tui/roadmap.go): annotate soft edges distinctly from hard ones (e.g. "after: x1" alongside the existing "blocked by: x1" annotation); soft-deferred epics still render as ready — no new status glyphs.
- Tickboard web roadmap: mirror union layering and the After field in internal/tickboard/ui/src/stores/roadmap-compute.ts (client-side compute used by cloud boards), and render soft edges visually distinct (dashed connector / muted badge) in internal/tickboard/ui/src/components/roadmap-view.ts. GET /api/roadmap picks up After automatically via query.Roadmap serialization — verify, no server change expected.
- Skill docs (skills/ticks/ is the source of truth, never the installed copy): SKILL.md Roadmaps section — use --after for preferred sequence, reserve --blocked-by for true technical dependency; with parallel fronts, multiple epics can be ready at once and the close-out task fleshes out the next FEASIBLE epic in soft order. references/tk-commands.md: document --after on create/update. references/tick-patterns.md: note that sequencing chosen to avoid merge conflicts (epics touching the same files) is a hard dependency, not a preference — use blocked_by for it.
- Tickboard UI work follows CLAUDE.md: pnpm, ticks-* components, Catppuccin Mocha.

Deliverables: CLI/TUI soft-edge rendering; web roadmap soft-edge rendering with compute parity tests; skill doc updates for the preference-vs-dependency distinction.
